subject: Chemistry
title: Electron Spin: The Fourth Quantum Number
description: A 60-second lesson on Electron Spin: The Fourth Quantum Number.

hook.text: Electrons spin—but not like a top. Quantum spin is an intrinsic property with only two values: up or down.

buildup.text: In 1925, Uhlenbeck and Goudsmit proposed electron spin to explain why spectral lines split in magnetic fields.

discovery.text: Spin gives electrons a tiny magnetic moment. Each orbital holds exactly two electrons—one spin-up, one spin-down.

twist.text: The Pauli exclusion principle says no two electrons can share the same quantum numbers. Spin doubles orbital capacity.

climax.text: Without spin, atoms would be flat. The periodic table, chemical bonds, and all matter depend on this quantum property.

punchline.text: Up or down, nothing between. A binary property that shapes all of chemistry.

quiz.question: Why is electron spin important for chemistry?
options: It doubles orbital capacity and determines the structure of the periodic table
It creates the force that holds atoms together
It determines the color of each element
